mcqs.question: What does Newton's First Law of Motion state?
options: A. An object at rest will stay at rest unless acted upon by a force.
B. Every action has an equal and opposite reaction.
C. Force equals mass times acceleration.
D. Energy can neither be created nor destroyed.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of inertia?
options: A. A car speeding up.
B. A book lying on a table.
C. A ball being thrown.
D. A bicycle coming to a stop.

mcqs.question: Inertia depends on the ______ of an object.
options: A. Shape
B. Mass
C. Color
D. Volume

mcqs.question: Which scenario best illustrates Newton's First Law?
options: A. A person pushing a box.
B. A train moving at constant speed.
C. A rocket launching into space.
D. A ball rolling down a hill.

mcqs.question: Newton's First Law is also known as the Law of ______.
options: A. Gravity
B. Inertia
C. Motion
D. Acceleration

fill_in_the_blanks.question: According to Newton's First Law, an object in motion will stay in motion unless acted upon by a ______.
The property of an object to resist changes in its state of motion is called ______.
The First Law of Motion was formulated by ______.
Inertial frames of reference are those where Newton's First Law holds true, meaning that no ______ is acting on the object.
The greater the mass of an object, the greater its ______.

short_questions.question: Can an object be at rest and still have inertia?
Give an everyday example of Newton's First Law.
What role does friction play in Newton's First Law?
